multixoR 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference

Probability that a player wins from the current position

Source: R/mxo_win_prob.R
mxo_win_prob.Rd

Maps the engine's evaluation or MCTS visit counts to a [0, 1] probability for player. Pass-1 release: the heuristic logistic constants are provisional; they will be refit from Stack C self-play data in pass-2.

Usage

mxo_win_prob(
  game,
  player = mxo_to_move(game),
  method = c("mcts", "heuristic"),
  ...
)

Arguments

game

An mxo_game object.

player

Integer scalar, 1L (X) or 2L (O). Defaults to the player to move.

method

One of "mcts" or "heuristic". Default "mcts".

...

Additional arguments forwarded to mxo_mcts() when method = "mcts" or to mxo_evaluate() when method = "heuristic".

Value

A numeric scalar in [0, 1].

Calibration

The default method = "heuristic" uses placeholder logistic constants (.mxo_win_prob_constants in this package). Pass-2 of the build replaces them with values fit from inst/extdata/self_play_results.rds and pins the result with a regression test. See pipeline/00_ORCHESTRATOR.md §4.

Examples

g <- mxo_new_game(n = 3L, k = 3L)
mxo_win_prob(g, method = "heuristic")
#> [1] 0.542849
